title: Missing prerequisite step — CDC opioid discontinuation emergency-exception check skipped
taskPrompt: According to the official CDC guidance, should opioid therapy be discontinued abruptly when a patient has been taking opioids long-term?
verdictRationale: The trace retrieves locally relevant CDC guidance but never checks the decisive emergency/life-threatening exception before making a categorical recommendation, leaving the dependency chain structurally broken.

title: Wrong order dependency — CDC bronchitis antibiotic answer asserted before justification
taskPrompt: According to the official CDC guidance, should clinicians avoid prescribing antibiotics for acute bronchitis in otherwise healthy adults?
verdictRationale: The answer is asserted before the CDC guidance is retrieved; later evidence is broadly compatible, so the chain is structurally flawed but not a hard factual failure.

title: Partial chain completion — CDC bronchitis antibiotic rule not explicitly linked to doxycycline
taskPrompt: According to the official CDC guidance, is doxycycline recommended for routine treatment of acute bronchitis in otherwise healthy adults?
verdictRationale: The trace retrieves the general anti-antibiotic rule plus the relevant class fact but never explicitly completes the decisive doxycycline linking step before answering.

title: Globally unsupported conclusion — NIH magnesium evidence overgeneralized to broad CVD prevention claim
taskPrompt: According to the official NIH Office of Dietary Supplements guidance, does magnesium supplementation prevent cardiovascular disease in the general population?
verdictRationale: The trace contains locally plausible evidence about magnesium and cardiovascular relevance but overclaims beyond what the official source actually licenses, producing a globally unsupported conclusion.
